data.title: Bitcoin's Potential for Volatility Amid Technical Indicators
data.description:
Bitcoin (BTC) is poised for increased volatility as several technical indicators suggest a significant upward movement in its price. According to Cointelegraph, the Bollinger Bands indicator, a tool used by traders to evaluate momentum and volatility, is signaling a potential breakout. The BTC price must surpass the resistance level at $80,000 to continue its upward trajectory.

The Bollinger Bands have reached their tightest point ever on the monthly time frame, indicating that volatility is imminent. Analyst Cantonese Cat highlighted this in a recent post, suggesting that the expansion of these bands could lead to a powerful price movement. Historically, similar patterns have preceded substantial bull runs, such as the 230% increase between December 2023 and August 2025, and the rallies in 2020 and 2016, which saw BTC prices surge by over 520% and 4,400%, respectively.

Coinvo Trading has also noted that Bitcoin's monthly RSI has dropped to its lowest level since late 2022, coinciding with a drop to a multi-year support trend line. This pattern has previously marked Bitcoin's macro bottoms, as seen during the 2022 bear market, which was followed by a 350% price rally to an all-time high of $73,800 in March 2024. Coinvo Trading suggests that a bull run is imminent, supported by a bullish MACD crossover on the weekly chart.

Bitcoin's recent 6% rally over the past three days has filled the $74,000-$77,000 CME gap created over the weekend. Traders are now focusing on the next CME gap above $80,000, formed in early February. Michael van de Poppe, founder of MC Capital, noted that resistance at $79,000 could temporarily halt Bitcoin's upward momentum, predicting a test of this level followed by a potential push to $86,000.

Additionally, Bitcoin's whale order book indicates significant sell pressure between $78,000-$80,000, emphasizing the importance of this resistance level. A close above the $76,000-$78,000 resistance zone would confirm buyer control, paving the way for a potential rally to $84,000. As Cointelegraph reported, overcoming these resistance levels is crucial for Bitcoin's continued upward movement.

data.title: Bitcoin's Fundamentals Adjust Amid BTCFi Ecosystem Contraction
data.description: Bitcoin's fundamental indicators have been adjusted due to the contraction of the BTCFi ecosystem. According to PANews, despite these adjustments, the macroeconomic environment continues to provide support for Bitcoin. The changes in the ecosystem have led to a reevaluation of Bitcoin's fundamental metrics, but broader economic factors remain favorable for its stability.

data.title: Volvo CEO: EX60 Orders Surpass Expectations, Production to Reach 40,000 Units This Year
data.description: Volvo Cars CEO has announced that the demand for the EX60 model has exceeded initial expectations. According to Jin10, the company is now focused on increasing production capacity to meet this demand. The CEO projected that nearly 40,000 units of the EX60 will be produced by the end of this year.

data.title: Bitcoin's monthly Bollinger Bands have reached their narrowest level in history, potentially signaling strong volatility.
data.description: Bitcoin's monthly Bollinger Bands have reached their narrowest level ever, indicating strong price volatility is imminent. Analyst Cantonese Cat stated that the expansion after a Bollinger Band contraction typically leads to dramatic price movements. Historical data shows that similar indicator performance in 2016 and 2020 triggered gains exceeding 4400% and 520%, respectively. Furthermore, Bitcoin's monthly RSI has fallen to its lowest level since the end of 2022, touching a macro support line. Currently, Bitcoin needs to break through the $80,000 resistance level to confirm the uptrend. On-chain data shows significant selling pressure from whales in the $78,000 to $80,000 range. Michael van de Poppe, founder of MC Capital, suggested that Bitcoin may experience a slight pullback after testing $79,000, before potentially testing $86,000.

data.title: KelpDAO attackers used a cross-chain bridge to transfer ETH, exchanged it for USDT, and then transferred it to Tron.
data.description: According to PeckShieldAlert, KelpDAO attackers bridged ETH from Ethereum to Arbitrum via AcrossProtocol, exchanged it for USDT, and then transferred the funds to trondao via LayerZero.

data.title: Iran Seizes Two Ships in Strait of Hormuz Following Ceasefire Extension
data.description: Iran has reportedly seized two ships in the strategic Strait of Hormuz, a critical waterway for global oil shipments. CNN Breaking News posted on X, highlighting the development shortly after U.S. President Donald Trump announced an indefinite extension of the ceasefire. The Strait of Hormuz is a vital passage, with a significant portion of the world's oil supply passing through it daily. The seizure of the ships has raised concerns about potential disruptions in oil transportation and heightened tensions in the region. The international community is closely monitoring the situation, as any escalation could have far-reaching implications for global energy markets and geopolitical stability. Further details about the seized vessels and the circumstances surrounding the incident remain limited at this time.

data.title: South Korean Customs Officer Accused of Bribery for Crypto Purchases
data.description: A customs police unit leader in South Korea has been accused by prosecutors of accepting $98,000 in bribes to finance personal cryptocurrency investments. According to NS3.AI, the officer allegedly promised to dismiss cases or prevent arrests in return for monetary compensation. Authorities are currently examining other cases managed by the officer to determine if similar misconduct occurred.

data.title: Turkey to Leverage COP31 Hosting Role to Promote Clean Energy
data.description: Turkey is set to utilize its position as the host of this year's COP31 climate summit to enhance the adoption of clean energy solutions. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting Turkey's strategic focus on renewable energy as part of its broader environmental agenda. The country aims to showcase its commitment to sustainable practices and encourage global cooperation in addressing climate change challenges. As the host nation, Turkey plans to lead discussions on innovative energy solutions and foster international partnerships to accelerate the transition to a low-carbon economy. The summit will serve as a platform for Turkey to demonstrate its advancements in clean energy technologies and its dedication to reducing carbon emissions. By hosting COP31, Turkey seeks to position itself as a leader in the global effort to combat climate change and promote sustainable development.

data.title: Emerging-Market Assets Decline Amid Rising Oil Prices and Stalled Iran Peace Talks
data.description: Emerging-market assets experienced a downturn as rising oil prices and stalled peace talks with Iran dampened investor risk appetite. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the impact of geopolitical tensions and fluctuating energy costs on market sentiment. The lack of progress in negotiations with Iran has contributed to uncertainty, affecting investment decisions in emerging markets. As oil prices continue to rise, concerns over inflation and economic stability are mounting, further influencing market dynamics. Investors are closely monitoring developments in the Middle East and energy markets, seeking clarity on future trends.

data.title: Boeing's Q1 2026 Revenue Exceeds Market Expectations
data.description: Boeing reported a revenue of $22.2 billion for the first quarter of 2026, surpassing market expectations of $21.99 billion. According to Jin10, this marks an increase from the $19.5 billion recorded in the same period last year.

data.title: DeFi Technologies' Subsidiary Secures $11 Million Institutional Investment
data.description: DeFi Technologies announced that its subsidiary, Valour Inc., has secured approximately $11 million in institutional investment. According to Foresight News, the funding was obtained through two transactions, including $10 million from the Frankfurt Stock Exchange and $1 million from the Swedish Spotlight Exchange. These investments highlight the growing institutional demand for regulated Hedera (HBAR) investment products, providing access to digital assets through traditional market infrastructure.

data.title: Gold and silver prices saw a slight pullback, while trading activity in Gate.com metals contracts increased significantly.
data.description: International gold and silver prices saw a slight pullback. Gold (XAUT) fell slightly by 1.10% in the last 24 hours, currently trading at $4,752.65; silver (XAG) fell by 1.93%, currently trading at $77.78. Despite signs of a temporary easing in the international situation, market risk aversion remains strong, driving active trading and open interest in related contracts. According to CoinGlass data, Gate XAU contracts saw a 24-hour trading volume of $197 million, a 68.68% increase compared to the previous period, with open interest reaching $22.11 million; Gate XAG contracts saw a 24-hour trading volume of $143 million, a significant 108.06% increase compared to the previous period, with open interest reaching $15.3 million. Gate pioneered a metals contract trading platform, offering 24/7 uninterrupted trading, providing users with greater strategic flexibility and asset management efficiency in volatile market conditions. Gate contracts cover a wide range of traditional financial assets, including stocks, metals, forex, indices, and commodities, supporting trading in core assets such as gold, silver, and popular global stocks. Gate continues to build a more efficient and professional one-stop trading platform for multi-asset trading for users worldwide.

data.title: Tencent and Alibaba in Talks to Invest in DeepSeek at Over $20 Billion Valuation
data.description: Tencent and Alibaba are reportedly in discussions to invest in DeepSeek, a company valued at over $20 billion. According to Foresight News, this information was initially reported by The Information. The potential investment highlights the growing interest of major tech companies in expanding their portfolios with promising ventures.

data.title: KelpDAO Attacker Transfers Funds Across Multiple Networks
data.description: According to Foresight News, PeckShield monitoring has revealed that the KelpDAO attacker transferred ETH from Ethereum to Arbitrum using the Across Protocol. The attacker then converted the funds to USDT0 and moved them to the Tron network via LayerZero.

data.title: STOCKS | Alibaba Shares Rise Over 1% in Pre-Market Trading
data.description: Alibaba's stock experienced a notable increase of over 1% during pre-market trading in the U.S. According to Jin10, this movement comes amid various market dynamics influencing investor sentiment. The rise in Alibaba's shares reflects ongoing interest and confidence in the company's performance and potential growth. Investors are closely monitoring the stock's trajectory as market conditions evolve.

data.title: India's Economic Growth Expected to Surpass US Markets in Five Years
data.description: India is projected to outperform US markets over the next five years, according to Grok. The forecast is based on India's anticipated GDP growth of 6.5% to 7% and corporate earnings growth of 12% to 15% annually. According to NS3.AI, Grok reiterated India's position as its top pick, highlighting the Reserve Bank of India's repo rate at 5.25%, a Consumer Price Index forecast of 4.6%, and a fiscal deficit target of 4.3%.

data.title: Trump Administration's Business Focus in Venezuela May Lead to Strategic Error
data.description: The Trump administration's emphasis on securing business advantages in Venezuela, rather than promoting political reform, could result in a significant strategic misstep. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting concerns that prioritizing economic interests over political change might undermine long-term U.S. objectives in the region. The administration's approach has sparked debate among experts, who argue that a balanced strategy addressing both economic and political aspects is crucial for sustainable progress in Venezuela. Critics warn that neglecting political reform could lead to instability and hinder efforts to establish a stable and democratic government. The situation remains complex, with ongoing discussions about the best path forward for U.S. involvement in Venezuela.

data.title: DeFi Users Seek High Yields with Strategy's STRC Dividend
data.description: DeFi users are actively routing Strategy's 11.5% STRC dividend through various protocols to achieve yields up to 39%. According to NS3.AI, platforms like Apyx, Saturn, and xStocks have allocated approximately $136 million, $85 million, and $53 million of STRC on-chain, respectively. Pendle reported that yield accrual on YT-sUSDat was paused after the STRC ex-dividend event on April 14, which caused the exchange rate to fall below its watermark.

data.title: Concerns Rise Over Iran's Strait of Hormuz Toll Charges
data.description:
On April 22, commodity traders expressed concerns about Iran's toll charges on the Strait of Hormuz, fearing it sets a dangerous precedent for global trade. According to BlockBeats, since the outbreak of the Middle East conflict in late February, Iran has attempted to impose transit fees on ships passing through the strait, sparking strong protests from international shipping regulators.

Larry Johnson, Global Shipping Director at Mercuria Energy Trading, stated, "A highly dangerous precedent is being set here, which undermines the right of innocent vessels to pass freely." He questioned the implications if other critical channels or waterways start imposing fees or face disruptions, citing potential scenarios in places like the Black Sea, the Danish Strait, and the Strait of Malacca. Johnson emphasized that the free flow of global trade is now compromised.

data.title: Gensyn Launches Decentralized AI Platform Delphi
data.description:
Gensyn has introduced its flagship product, Delphi, a decentralized information marketplace platform powered by AI settlement. According to ChainCatcher, this platform allows creators to establish markets and earn a 1.5% fee based on transaction volume upon successful settlement.

Currently, Delphi is open for trading to all users, though market creation is restricted to invited creators. The platform is expected to launch on the mainnet in the coming weeks. Built on Gensyn's Ethereum Layer 2 network, Delphi utilizes verifiable smart oracles for settlement. Gensyn reports that since its testnet launch in December 2025, Delphi has recorded millions of dollars in transaction volume.
